Programme: MUT (Mangosuthu University of Technology) uses the standard NSC 7-point achievement level scale: L8=90-100%; L7=80-89%; L6=70-79%; L5=60-69%; L4=50-59%; L3=40-49%; L2=30-39%; L1=0-29%. APS is calculated from the best 6 subjects presented. Life Orientation is considered a non-curriculum subject (excluded from APS). Minimum entrance requirements: NSC with Diploma pass (or N3 with 50% in Maths and English); minimum 6 subjects at L4 for Diploma study; L3 for Access/Bridging courses; English HL or FAL at L4. Meeting minimum requirements does NOT guarantee acceptance - competition is keen with over 40 000 applications per year. Some departments administer a compulsory entrance test. Apply via CAO (www.cao.ac.za); R110 CAO application fee. Closing dates: Engineering 1st semester = 30 Sept; Engineering 2nd semester = 31 May; Natural Sciences = 30 Sept; Management Sciences = 30 Sept. Two campuses in Umlazi: Main Campus (Engineering and Management Sciences) and Natural Sciences Campus. BSc Environmental Health and BHSc-MLS require a Bachelor pass (not just Diploma pass). MathLit note: Engineering programmes do not list MathLit as an accepted alternative - Maths required. Source is 2023 prospectus.
